title: History of Python
description: Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. The language emphasizes readability, simplicity, and explicitness — famously captured in the Zen of Python.

timeline.event: Python 3.0 — print function, unicode, breaking changes
Python 3.10 — match statement, better error messages
Python 3.12 — faster CPython, improved error messages
Python 3.13 — JIT compiler, free-threaded mode experimental
Python 3.13+ continues performance and no-GIL iteration

details: Python was conceived in the late 1980s as a successor to the ABC language. Guido van Rossum, then working at Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica (CWI), wanted a language that bridged the gap between C and shell scripting. The name 'Python' honors Monty Python's Flying Circus, not the snake. Python 2.0 introduced list comprehensions and a garbage collector, while Python 3.0 (2008) was a deliberate break from backward compatibility to fix fundamental design flaws. The transition from Python 2 to 3 took over a decade. Today, Python is one of the most popular languages worldwide, driven by its dominance in data science, machine learning, web development (Django, Flask), and automation.
